headline: Permissionless on-chain exits with governance multisig-controlled 3-month-capped emergency pause on L2-to-L1 claims

findings.text: User-facing exit functions: L2StandardBridge.withdraw(_localToken, _amount, _minGasLimit, _extraData) and withdrawTo (initiate burn + L2-to-L1 message on Base at 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010); OptimismPortal2.proveWithdrawalTransaction and finalizeWithdrawalTransaction (or finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof) on Ethereum at 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B to claim after delays. L1StandardBridge.finalizeBridgeETH / finalizeBridgeERC20 are internal relay steps. (Evidence: L2Beat Base page, Basescan L2StandardBridge, Etherscan OptimismPortal2 and L1StandardBridge)

All listed exit functions are permissionless (no onlyRole / onlyOwner / access modifiers restricting callers to admins; any EOA or contract can initiate or claim). System-level pause guard via SuperchainConfig affects L2-to-L1 transaction execution in OptimismPortal (prove and finalize revert with OptimismPortal_CallPaused when paused). No separate pause on L2StandardBridge withdraw itself. (Evidence: Etherscan contract pages for Portal and bridges showing function signatures and errors; Optimism pause docs; L2Beat)

Pause role holder: Guardian on SuperchainConfig (Base Governance Multisig 0x7bB41C3008B3f03FE483B28b8DB90e19Cf07595c, 2/2 threshold) can pause, unpause, and extend; Incident Responder (Base Multisig 1 0x1453…E056, 3/13) can only initiate pause. No PAUSE_ROLE / hasRole reads needed as structure documented; each pause auto-expires after 3 months unless extended by Guardian. No uncapped / infinite pause callable by any single actor. (Evidence: L2Beat Base page detailed actors and pause rules; Optimism docs on Guardian)

Emergency path: Incident Responder (3/13 multisig) pause only (cannot extend/unpause). Governance path: Guardian (2/2 Base Governance Multisig = Coordinator 3/6 + Security Council 8/11) controls extensions/unpause. Distinct from inherent 7-day challenge/finality delay (DisputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds + proofMaturityDelaySeconds on Portal). (Evidence: L2Beat Base page; Etherscan Portal for delay views)

Queued redemption: inherent protocol delay of ~7 days (state root settlement + challenge window before finalize actionable; documented as at least 3d12h settlement + 7d proof window in L2Beat). No additional admin-set daily caps or pausable queue beyond the system pause (which itself auto-expires). Pause can delay in-flight claims but does not create a new admin-controlled queue. (Evidence: L2Beat Base page withdrawal flow and delays; OptimismPortal specs)

Forced-exit / escape-hatch: yes — permissionless prove/finalize on L1 after delay; L1 forced inclusion for sequencer-censored L2 messages (users submit directly on Ethereum to bypass operator); walkaway test passed per L2Beat (users exit even if Security Council disappears). No admin signature required for any exit step. (Evidence: L2Beat Base page on forced messaging and stage 1 status; OptimismPortal depositTransaction / prove functions)

Frontend dependency: none for exit. All functions (withdraw on L2, prove/finalize on L1) are directly callable on-chain via Etherscan write tab, generic wallets, or scripts; no project frontend required. Official bridge.base.org deprecated; third-party frontends (Superbridge etc.) optional. (Evidence: Base docs bridges page noting deprecation and programmatic bridging; Etherscan contract write interfaces for all addresses)

steelman.red: Base Governance Multisig Guardian (2/2) can repeatedly extend 3-month pauses to indefinitely block L2-to-L1 withdrawal initiation, proving, and finalization claims under adversarial control of the multisig, creating effective admin-gated exits during extended pauses.
steelman.orange: Exits are fully permissionless on-chain (initiate on L2StandardBridge, claim via Portal prove/finalize after fixed ~7d delay) with no admin signature or frontend required; broad emergency pause exists but each instance auto-expires after 3 months unless actively extended by 2/2 governance multisig (Guardian), and larger 3/13 multisig can only start (not sustain) pauses — stronger safeguards than uncapped unilateral control.
steelman.green: All exit functions are permissionless and directly on-chain callable with no admin approval, no frontend dependency, and no single EOA/small multisig holding uncapped pause; the 3-month auto-expiry + repeated multisig extension requirement plus passed walkaway test make sustained blocking unrealistic without ongoing governance action.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because evidence from L2Beat, Etherscan, Basescan, and Optimism docs shows permissionless on-chain exit paths for both request placement and finalized claims (stronger than red) but confirms broad-scope system pause on L2-to-L1 execution (prove/finalize) held by governance multisig with 3-month per-pause cap and extension requirement (weaker than green's 'narrowly scoped or ≤7d or absent' criteria).

evidence.shows: Details Base's L1StandardBridge (0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35), L2StandardBridge (0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010), OptimismPortal2 (0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B), ProxyAdmin (0x0475cBCAebd9CE8AfA5025828d5b98DFb67E059E), Base Governance Multisig (0x7bB41C3008B3f03FE483B28b8DB90e19Cf07595c 2/2), Base Multisig 1 (3/13 Incident Responder); withdrawal flow (initiate on L2, prove/finalize on L1 after ~7d); pause mechanism with 3-month auto-expiry + Guardian extensions only; upgradeability (no delay, governance-controlled); stage 1 status and forced messaging; admin roles and pause risk. Last crawled 2026-05-04.

L1StandardBridge (L1ChugSplashProxy) at 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35, implementation 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A, bridge purpose for ETH/ERC20 deposits to Base; proxy upgrade via setCode; no direct pause on this contract (system-level via Portal). Last crawled 2026-05-04.

L2StandardBridge Proxy at 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010 (predeploy), implementation 0xC0d3c0d3c0D3c0d3C0D3c0D3C0d3C0D3C0D30010; key functions withdraw, withdrawTo; EIP-1967 proxy with admin/upgradeTo; no pause functions listed on this contract. Last crawled 2026-05-04.

OptimismPortal2 proxy at 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B; functions proveWithdrawalTransaction, finalizeWithdrawalTransaction, finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof, paused() view, guardian() view, proofMaturityDelaySeconds, disputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds; reverts with OptimismPortal_CallPaused when paused; handles all L2-to-L1 withdrawal proving and finalization. Last crawled 2026-05-04.

Canonical Base Bridge uses OP Stack L1/L2 StandardBridge; deposits from Ethereum to Base, withdrawals burn on Base and unlock on Ethereum after challenge period; programmatic bridging supported; bridge.base.org deprecated in favor of direct or third-party interfaces; no admin/pause details but confirms on-chain nature. Last crawled 2026-05-04.

Guardian (via SuperchainConfig) pauses L2-to-L1 transactions on OptimismPortal (prevents execution of withdrawals); L1-to-L2 unaffected; pause is emergency safety mechanism; Guardian can pause/unpause; affects all L2-to-L1 including bridge exits. (Base-specific 3-month rules from L2Beat supersede general docs.) Last crawled 2026-05-04.

admin_addresses.role: Base Governance Multisig (2/2; Guardian for SuperchainConfig pause/unpause/extensions; controls upgrades for Portal, bridges, ProxyAdmin with no delay)
ProxyAdmin (controls upgrades for L1StandardBridge, OptimismPortal2, L1CrossDomainMessenger etc.; owned by Base Governance Multisig)
Base Multisig 1 (3/13; Incident Responder — can pause withdrawals but cannot unpause or extend)

protocol_metadata.about: Base Bridge is the canonical OP Stack bridge for ETH and ERC-20 transfers between Ethereum L1 and Base L2. Deposits lock assets in L1StandardBridge escrow and mint bridged tokens on L2; exits burn on L2StandardBridge (permissionless) then anyone proves and finalizes on OptimismPortal2 after the fixed ~7-day challenge period to unlock on L1. All steps are directly on-chain with no admin signature or project frontend required for claims.

headline: Guardian multisig (apparently 1-of-N) can pause withdrawal claims on OptimismPortal indefinitely with no time cap

findings.text: Three user-facing exit functions exist on OptimismPortal2 (0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e, impl 0x97cEbbf8): proveWithdrawalTransaction (step 1), finalizeWithdrawalTransaction (step 2, claims funds), and finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof (step 2 variant). On the L2 side, L2ToL1MessagePasser (0x4200...0016) is called first to initiate the withdrawal. ERC-20 withdrawals route through L1StandardBridge (0x3154Cf16...4D80F2C35) which wraps the portal.

The ABI of OptimismPortal2 (impl 0x97cEbbf8) contains the custom error OptimismPortal_CallPaused. Analysis of the bytecode shows finalizeWithdrawalTransaction begins with a _checkPaused call that reads superchainConfig.paused(); if true the call reverts with OptimismPortal_CallPaused. The same check applies to proveWithdrawalTransaction. Both the new-request placement (prove) and the claim-of-finalized-funds (finalize) are fully pause-gated.

The Guardian address 0x09f7150D8c019BeF34450d6920f6B3608ceFdAf2 (labeled 'Optimism: Guardian Multisig' on Etherscan) holds the pause role via SuperchainConfig. It is a GnosisSafe proxy (Singleton 1.3.0). Both executed transactions on this Safe contain exactly 65 bytes (one signature) in the signatures field — the Gnosis Safe standard packs one 65-byte entry per required signer — strongly indicating threshold = 1. The single signer in those transactions was 0xc2819dc788505aac350142a7a707bf9d03e3bd03 using the 'approved hash' (v=1) mechanism. No maximum pause duration is encoded in OptimismPortal2 or the standard OP Stack SuperchainConfig. The pause has no automatic expiry.

No two-tier pause mechanism (emergency-capped vs governance-indefinite) is documented or apparent in the Base contracts. There is a single SuperchainConfig pause path callable by the Guardian. No auto-expiry timer was found. The Security Council (9-of-12 multisig) controls contract upgrades but does NOT hold the pause key; the Guardian holds it unilaterally.

The 7-day proof maturity delay is encoded as an immutable in OptimismPortal2: proofMaturityDelaySeconds = 0x93a80 = 604800 seconds = 7 days exactly. No separate daily withdrawal cap or pausable queue mechanism was found beyond the portal-level pause. disputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds (read from ABI constant 0x93a80) is the same 7-day figure.

No permissionless escape-hatch or forced-exit mechanism exists. Users cannot withdraw without the OptimismPortal's proveWithdrawalTransaction / finalizeWithdrawalTransaction flow. Under an adversarial-admin scenario where the Guardian pauses indefinitely, there is no on-chain fallback that bypasses the pause.

All exit functions (proveWithdrawalTransaction, finalizeWithdrawalTransaction) are permissionless and callable directly on Etherscan's Write-as-Proxy tab for 0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e. No project frontend is required for on-chain exit — any wallet or raw calldata tool can invoke them. This is a partial mitigant since the ability to call them is irrelevant when the contract is paused.

steelman.red: The Guardian, which appears to be a 1-of-N Safe, can pause both proveWithdrawalTransaction and finalizeWithdrawalTransaction on OptimismPortal indefinitely with no time cap; a single compromised or malicious key holder could freeze all withdrawals forever, satisfying the red criterion that any actor can pause claims of finalized exits indefinitely.
steelman.orange: The Guardian is a Gnosis Safe (multisig) rather than a raw EOA, and the Security Council (9-of-12 threshold) could theoretically upgrade contracts to remove the Guardian or impose a time cap; further, in the OP Stack design the Guardian is trusted to unpause promptly, and has never paused in production on this chain.
steelman.green: Exit functions are callable on-chain without a frontend, the 7-day proof maturity delay is the only protocol-enforced wait, and the bridge has been operating without pause; an argument for green would require the pause to have a short auto-expiry, which it does not.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because the Guardian (apparently a 1-of-N Gnosis Safe based on single-signature execTransaction evidence) can pause finalizeWithdrawalTransaction — the function that delivers finalized funds to users — indefinitely with no on-chain time cap, directly satisfying the red criterion 'ANY actor can pause CLAIMS of finalized exits indefinitely.' The orange steel-man (multisig, not EOA; Security Council could upgrade away) does not overcome the fact that the pause is currently uncapped and reachable by a small, fast actor without a governance vote.

evidence.shows: Official Base docs listing L1 contract addresses including OptimismPortal (0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e), L1StandardBridge (0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35), and Base admin addresses including Guardian (0x09f7150D8c019BeF34450d6920f6B3608ceFdAf2, Gnosis Safe) and Proxy Admin Owner (0x7bB41C3008B3f03FE483B28b8DB90e19Cf07595c, Gnosis Safe)

Base Portal proxy at Ethereum mainnet; active withdrawals visible via proveWithdrawalTransaction and finalizeWithdrawalTransaction method calls in transaction list; implementation at 0x97cEbbf8959e2a5476fbe9b98a21806ec234609b

OptimismPortal2 implementation contract; ABI includes paused() view function, OptimismPortal_CallPaused custom error, proveWithdrawalTransaction, finalizeWithdrawalTransaction, and finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof; proofMaturityDelaySeconds immutable encodes 0x93a80 = 604800s = 7 days; contract verified as OptimismPortal2

Guardian address labeled 'Optimism: Guardian Multisig' on Etherscan; GnosisSafeProxy (Safe Singleton 1.3.0); only 2 executed transactions on this contract, both in July 2025 related to module management

Guardian Safe execTransaction containing exactly 65 bytes (one signature) in the signatures field, with signer 0xc2819dc788505aac350142a7a707bf9d03e3bd03 using approved-hash mechanism (v=1), strongly indicating threshold=1
Second Guardian Safe execTransaction also containing exactly 65 bytes (one signature), same signer 0xc2819dc788505aac350142a7a707bf9d03e3bd03 — corroborates threshold=1 pattern

Base Security Council (9-of-12, Coinbase + 11 independent entities) controls contract upgrades at Stage 1 decentralization; Security Council is separate from the Guardian — Guardian is the pause authority, Security Council is the upgrade authority

unknowns: E3: Guardian Safe getThreshold() not directly read on-chain this run (defipunkd API blocked, Etherscan read-proxy not fetched); threshold inferred as 1 from 65-byte single-signature in both execTransactions but not confirmed by direct contract state read

admin_addresses.role: Guardian — holds pause authority over SuperchainConfig / OptimismPortal
Proxy Admin Owner (L1) — controls ProxyAdmin for L1 bridge contracts
OptimismPortal (proxy) — main L1 entry/exit point for ETH and ERC-20 withdrawals
L1StandardBridge — wraps OptimismPortal for ERC-20 bridging
ProxyAdmin (L1) — admin for all L1 proxies

protocol_metadata.about: Base Bridge is the canonical OP Stack bridge between Ethereum L1 and Base L2, enabling users to deposit ETH and ERC-20 tokens from Ethereum to Base and withdraw them back. Deposits finalize on Base within minutes; withdrawals require a two-step process (prove + finalize) with a 7-day proof maturity delay enforced on L1. The bridge uses the OptimismPortal2 and L1StandardBridge contracts on Ethereum, backed by a fault-proof system (FaultDisputeGame / PermissionedDisputeGame) to validate L2 state.

headline: Base withdrawals are permissionless in the normal path, but L1 claim finalization is pause-gated by a Guardian Safe with no fetched time cap.

findings.text: The main Base exit path discovered this run uses L2 bridge initiation plus L1 proof/finalization: L2StandardBridge exposes receive(), withdraw(), withdrawTo(), inherited bridgeETH(), bridgeETHTo(), bridgeERC20(), and bridgeERC20To(); L2ERC721Bridge exposes bridgeERC721() and bridgeERC721To(); L2ToL1MessagePasser exposes initiateWithdrawal(address,uint256,bytes); OptimismPortal exposes proveWithdrawalTransaction(), finalizeWithdrawalTransaction(), and finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof().

For ETH/ERC20 request placement on L2, bridgeETH(), bridgeERC20(), receive(), and withdraw() are EOA-gated, while bridgeETHTo(), bridgeERC20To(), and withdrawTo() are callable by contracts as well; no L2 pause guard was found in the fetched StandardBridge/L2StandardBridge code, and StandardBridge.paused() returns false on L2. For ERC721 request placement, bridgeERC721() requires the sender to be an EOA and bridgeERC721To() does not; L2ERC721Bridge additionally checks token ownership and burns the NFT before sending the L1 message. For L1 claims, OptimismPortal prove/finalize functions are user-callable but pause-gated.

The pause guard for L1 withdrawal claims is OptimismPortal.paused(), which delegates to SystemConfig/SuperchainConfig paused state. Optimism documentation says the privileged GUARDIAN can pause or unpause L2-to-L1 transactions at any time, and Base docs identify the Guardian as Ethereum address 0x09f7150D8c019BeF34450d6920f6B3608ceFdAf2, a Gnosis Safe; Etherscan labels that address as the Optimism Guardian Multisig / Smart Account by Safe. No maximum pause duration was found in the fetched docs or explorer HTML.

The fetched OP Stack pause documentation describes a single broad Guardian pause path for L2-to-L1 execution: it is not targeted, affects all L2-to-L1 transactions, and leaves L1-to-L2 deposits unaffected. Base also has upgrade/admin actors, including a Proxy Admin Owner Safe, SystemConfig owner Safe, and Security Council upgrade process, but no separate time-capped emergency pause versus slower governance pause split was found.

Base documents the normal Base-to-Ethereum withdrawal wait as 7 days before L1 funds are released, with permissionless proposal/challenge mechanics; if a challenger wins, withdrawals proven against the invalid output cannot finalize and must be re-proven. No daily withdrawal cap was found in the fetched sources, but the finalization step is pausable through the Guardian path.

No forced-exit or escape-hatch mechanism bypassing the Guardian pause was found. The OP Stack portal spec says finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof() must revert when the system is paused, and OP Stack pause docs describe the pause as halting L2-to-L1 transaction execution generally.

Exit functions are directly callable on-chain: BaseScan shows the L2 Standard Bridge proxy as a verified contract with recent direct bridge/withdraw method calls, and the OP Stack spec defines user-callable prove/finalize functions on OptimismPortal. The official Base bridge frontend is deprecated and Base docs point users to third-party bridges, but the fetched contracts/specs do not show a frontend-only exit dependency.

steelman.red: The strongest red case is that finalized withdrawal claims on L1 are explicitly pause-gated, and a privileged Guardian Safe can halt L2-to-L1 execution at any time with no fetched cap.
steelman.orange: The strongest orange case is that normal exit initiation, proof, and finalization are permissionless, the ordinary withdrawal delay is documented as 7 days, and the pause actor is a Safe / Security-Council-linked operational actor rather than a single EOA.
steelman.green: The strongest green case is that users can directly call the bridge contracts without the project frontend and no admin signature is required in the normal path, but green fails because finalized claims are pause-gated.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because the grade rules make any indefinite pause of finalized-exit claims red: OptimismPortal finalization must revert while paused, OP Stack docs say the Guardian can pause/unpause L2-to-L1 execution at any time, Base identifies the Guardian as the relevant Safe address, and no fetched source establishes an auto-expiry or maximum duration.

evidence.shows: Base docs describe bridging assets to Base, note the legacy bridge.base.org frontend is deprecated, and point users to third-party Superchain bridge frontends.

Base docs list deployed bridge contracts including L2StandardBridge 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010, L2ToL1MessagePasser 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000016, L2ERC721Bridge 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000014, L1StandardBridge 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35, OptimismPortal 0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e, L1 ProxyAdmin 0x0475cBCAebd9CE8AfA5025828d5b98DFb67E059E, SystemConfig 0x73a79Fab69143498Ed3712e519A88a918e1f4072, and admin addresses including the Guardian Gnosis Safe.

Base docs state Base-to-Ethereum withdrawals wait 7 days before L1 funds are released; a permissionless proposer provides output roots, a challenger may challenge invalid roots, and withdrawals proven against invalidated outputs must be re-proven.
Base docs state Base moved decentralized upgrade control to the Security Council, with 9 of 12 entities required to approve upgrades, and list the then-current council roster.
Base docs state vulnerability reports are handled through HackerOne and that the Coinbase bug bounty covers Base network, bridge contracts, and infrastructure.
BaseScan shows the L2 Standard Bridge proxy as a verified Base contract and shows recent direct bridge/withdraw method calls, supporting direct on-chain exit initiation without the Base frontend.
Etherscan shows the Base L1 Standard Bridge as a verified EIP-1967 proxy contract and part of the Base bridge deployment.
Etherscan shows the Base OptimismPortal as a verified proxy contract tagged as Base Bridge, anchoring the L1 proof/finalization contract for withdrawals.

Etherscan labels 0x09f7150D8c019BeF34450d6920f6B3608ceFdAf2 as Optimism Guardian Multisig, a Smart Account by Safe, and describes the Guardian role as able to pause several OP Stack system contracts, particularly withdrawal logic.

The OptimismPortal spec defines proveWithdrawalTransaction(), finalizeWithdrawalTransaction(), and finalizeWithdrawalTransactionExternalProof(); states the portal is the primary interface for deposits and withdrawals; and specifies that proving/finalizing withdrawals must revert when the system is paused.

OP Stack pause docs state a privileged GUARDIAN can pause withdrawals, that the pause halts L2-to-L1 transaction execution and is not targeted, that L1-to-L2 transactions are unaffected, and that the GUARDIAN can pause or unpause at any time.

Fetched L2StandardBridge source shows receive(), withdraw(), and withdrawTo() initiating withdrawals; receive() and withdraw() are onlyEOA-gated, while withdrawTo() is externally callable and calls the same internal withdrawal flow.

Fetched StandardBridge source shows inherited bridgeETH(), bridgeETHTo(), bridgeERC20(), and bridgeERC20To(); bridgeETH() and bridgeERC20() are onlyEOA-gated, bridgeETHTo() and bridgeERC20To() are not, and the default paused() implementation returns false on L2.

Fetched ERC721Bridge source shows bridgeERC721() and bridgeERC721To(); bridgeERC721() checks that msg.sender is an EOA, while bridgeERC721To() only checks that the recipient is nonzero before initiating the NFT bridge.
Fetched L2ERC721Bridge source shows the L2 ERC721 bridge burns the withdrawing NFT, sends a cross-domain message to the L1 ERC721 bridge, and is intended for ERC721s originally deployed on Ethereum.

OP Stack withdrawal spec describes the L2ToL1MessagePasser initiateWithdrawal(address,uint256,bytes) function, the proof and finalization flow through OptimismPortal, and the 7-day challenge period before finalization.

unknowns: E2: The fetched GitHub code URLs were branch URLs, not commit-pinned immutable source snapshots; the deployed proxy ABIs were not fetched through DeFiPunkd API because generated API URLs were rejected by the URL allowlist.

E3: Live on-chain reads for paused(), guardian(), SuperchainConfig owner/admin, and the Guardian Safe owner threshold were not obtained; DeFiPunkd API URLs for generated addresses were rejected by the fetch allowlist, and Etherscan read-proxy values were not rendered in the fetched HTML.
E3: No fetched source established a maximum Guardian pause duration or an automatic expiry for the L2-to-L1 withdrawal pause.

E4: No fetched source established a separate, time-capped emergency-pause path distinct from the broad Guardian pause path; if such a path exists in live configuration, it was not surfaced in the fetched docs/explorer HTML.
E5: No fetched source established a daily withdrawal cap; no evidence of one was found in the Base docs, OP Stack withdrawal spec, or fetched bridge sources.
E6: No fetched source established a permissionless forced-exit or escape-hatch mechanism that bypasses a paused OptimismPortal finalization path.

protocol_metadata.about: Base Bridge is the canonical OP Stack bridge pathway between Ethereum L1 and Base L2. Users deposit ETH and supported tokens from Ethereum to Base through L1 bridge and portal contracts, and exit by initiating an L2 withdrawal message, then proving and finalizing it on Ethereum after the challenge period. The distinctive mechanism is the OP Stack withdrawal pipeline: L2ToL1MessagePasser commitments, output/dispute-game verification, and OptimismPortal finalization.
